snippet.title: S. Korea's PM discuss cooperation in content industry with Netflix co-CEO
snippet.description:
한덕수 총리, 서랜도스 넷플릭스 CEO 면담.. OTT 시장 활성화 방안 논의

Prime Minister Han Duck-soo today met with Netflix co-CEO Ted Sarandos to discuss ways to expand Seoul-Washington cooperation in the content industry.
At Government Complex in Seoul, Prime Minister Han expressed high anticipation for Netflix's plan to invest 2-point-5 billion U.S. dollars in K-content over the next four years.
Mister Sarandos also said that he has been impressed by South Korea's vision in the content industry that was laid out by President Yoon during his speech at the U.S. Parliament.
The two sides vowed to continue efforts for the co-development of the content industry in both countries, as well as the vitalization of the streaming platform market.

snippet.title: CEO of Netflix resigns
snippet.description:
Co-founder and CEO of Netflix Reed Hastings has announced he will be stepping down from his roles.

Mr Hastings will shift to the new position of Executive Chairman.

snippet.title: Netflix's unwavering belief in power of stories proven in Korea: Netflix co-CEO Sarandos
snippet.description:
넷플릭스 CEO "K-콘텐츠 힘은 스토리텔링" 계정공유금지 시행 시사

Netflix co-CEO Ted Sarandos praised South Korea for proving the company's unwavering belief that great stories can come from anywhere and captivate audiences worldwide.
Speaking at a media event in Seoul on Thursday, he said Netflix has invested in original, authentic local stories in over 50 countries since its global launch in 2016, adding that 60 percent of Netflix subscribers had watched at least one Korean title.
He also said the global viewership of Korean content went up sixfold in the last four years citing the strength of its storytelling power.
Meanwhile, Netflix announced its policy of charging additional fees for accounts shared outside families will be implemented in South Korea soon.

snippet.title: NETFLIX: Streaming-Riese meldet Plus von 7,7 Millionen neuen Nutzern
snippet.description:
Beim Streaming-Riesen Netflix geht die Ära von Firmengründer Reed Hastings zu Ende. Nach mehr als zwei Jahrzehnten zog sich der 62-Jährige aus dem Top-Management zurück. Der Schritt folgte auf ein überraschend starkes Schlussquartal. Nicht zuletzt dank der Doku-Serie "Harry & Meghan" wuchs Netflix mit einem Plus von 7,7 Millionen Nutzern deutlich schneller als erwartet. Der Dienst ist im Wandel: Vor kurzem startete ein günstigerer Abo-Tarif mit Werbung, und demnächst wird gegen das Teilen von Passwörtern vorgegangen.

"Selbst Gründer müssen sich weiterentwickeln!", schrieb Hastings im Firmenblog. Er hatte 2020 den langjährigen Inhalte-Chef Ted Sarandos in eine Doppelspitze befördert. Jetzt rückt Greg Peters, der bisher das Tagesgeschäft verantwortete, zum Co-Chef auf. "Ehrlich gesagt haben die beiden mehr und mehr das Unternehmen geführt", sagte Hastings in einem Interview. Er dürfte als geschäftsführender Verwaltungsratsvorsitzender weiter großen Einfluss behalten.

snippet.title: The Hidden Truth Behind Streaming Numbers Revealed by Netflix Exec and JRE
snippet.description:
On "The Joe Rogan Experience", Joe discusses Sarandos' stance on not disclosing streaming numbers. Sarandos argues that, in the early days of original programming, creators felt confined by the pressure of overnight ratings. He suggests that by withholding these numbers, they're shielding creators from this stress. Joe, however, humorously points out the irony, suggesting that platforms might be doing it more for their own benefit rather than for the creators. The conversation sheds light on the lack of transparency in the streaming industry and its implications for content creators.
